title: The Futur Simple
description: A2 introduction to the futur simple: how to form it from the infinitive, the key irregular stems, and when to use it instead of the futur proche you met in A1. All explanations in English.

cards.front: parler → je parlerai, tu parleras, il parlera, nous parlerons, vous parlerez, ils parleront
cards.back: futur simple endings on a regular -er verb: -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont
cards.notes: The futur simple is built by adding the endings **-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont** directly to the **infinitive**. For **-er** and **-ir** verbs the infinitive is used as-is: *parler → parlera*, *finir → finira*. For **-re** verbs the final **-e** is dropped first: *prendre → prendr-* then *prendrai*.

cards.back: -re verbs: drop the final -e before adding the endings
cards.notes: For **-re** verbs, remove the **-e** at the end of the infinitive to get the stem, then attach the endings: *prendre → prendr-* → *je prendrai, tu prendras...*. Same for *vendre → vendr-*, *attendre → attendr-*, *répondre → répondr-*.
cards.hint: Drop the -e from -re verbs before adding the futur simple ending.

cards.front: être → ser-, avoir → aur-, aller → ir-, faire → fer-, venir → viendr-
cards.back: the five most common irregular futur stems
cards.notes: These verbs use a **completely different stem** (not the infinitive) in the futur simple. The endings are always the same: *-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont*. Examples: *je serai* (I will be), *j'aurai* (I will have), *j'irai* (I will go), *je ferai* (I will do/make), *je viendrai* (I will come).

cards.front: pouvoir → pourr-, voir → verr-, devoir → devr-, vouloir → voudr-, savoir → saur-
cards.back: five more common irregular futur stems
cards.notes: A second batch of high-frequency irregulars: *je pourrai* (I will be able to), *je verrai* (I will see), *je devrai* (I will have to), *je voudrai* (I will want), *je saurai* (I will know). Notice the double **-r-** in *pourrai* and *verrai* — a common spelling trap.
cards.hint: Watch the double -rr- in pourrai and verrai.

cards.front: Demain, il pleuvra. / Un jour, je voyagerai partout.
cards.back: futur simple = general/distant future, predictions, promises
cards.notes:
Use the **futur simple** for:
- **General or distant future** events: *L'annee prochaine, nous demenagerons.* (Next year, we will move.)
- **Predictions**: *Il pleuvra demain.* (It will rain tomorrow.)
- **Promises or firm intentions**: *Je t'appellerai ce soir.* (I will call you tonight.)

**Contrast with A1 futur proche** (aller + infinitive), which signals something happening **very soon** or already planned: *Je vais manger* (I am going to eat [right now/shortly]).

cards.front: Je vais partir. (futur proche) vs. Je partirai bientot. (futur simple)
cards.back: near future (going to) vs. general future (will)
cards.notes: In A1, **futur proche** (aller + infinitive) covered the near future: *Je vais partir* means 'I am going to leave' (imminent or planned). The **futur simple** — *Je partirai* — expresses a more general 'I will leave', used for predictions, promises, or more distant events. Both are correct French; the futur simple sounds slightly more formal or further away in time.

cards.back: to go → I will go (irregular stem: ir-)
cards.notes: *j'irai, tu iras, il ira, nous irons, vous irez, ils iront*. A very common error for English speakers is to write *j'allerai* — but the stem is **ir-**, not the infinitive.
cards.hint: The stem is ir-, NOT aller-. Never write j'allerai.

cards.back: to be → I will be (irregular stem: ser-)
cards.notes: *je serai, tu seras, il sera, nous serons, vous serez, ils seront*. Example: *Ce sera difficile.* (That will be difficult.)

cards.back: to have → I will have (irregular stem: aur-)
cards.notes: *j'aurai, tu auras, il aura, nous aurons, vous aurez, ils auront*. Example: *J'aurai le temps demain.* (I will have time tomorrow.)

cards.back: to do / make → I will do (irregular stem: fer-)
cards.notes: *je ferai, tu feras, il fera, nous ferons, vous ferez, ils feront*. Example: *Nous ferons un gateau.* (We will make a cake.)

cards.back: to come → I will come (irregular stem: viendr-)
cards.notes: *je viendrai, tu viendras, il viendra, nous viendrons, vous viendrez, ils viendront*. Example: *Elle viendra a la fete.* (She will come to the party.)

cards.back: to finish → I will finish (regular -ir verb, stem = infinitive)
cards.notes: *je finirai, tu finiras, il finira, nous finirons, vous finirez, ils finiront*. Regular -ir verbs keep the full infinitive as the stem: *finir + ai = finirai*.

steps.title: Forming the futur simple
steps.body:
# The Futur Simple

The futur simple is French's main way to say **will** — for predictions, promises, and general future events.

## The rule: infinitive + endings

For most verbs, simply attach these endings to the **infinitive**:

| Subject | Ending | parler | finir |
|---|---|---|---|
| je | **-ai** | parler**ai** | finir**ai** |
| tu | **-as** | parler**as** | finir**as** |
| il/elle | **-a** | parler**a** | finir**a** |
| nous | **-ons** | parler**ons** | finir**ons** |
| vous | **-ez** | parler**ez** | finir**ez** |
| ils/elles | **-ont** | parler**ont** | finir**ont** |

## Special rule for -re verbs

**-re verbs drop the final -e** before adding the endings:

| Infinitive | Stem | je form |
|---|---|---|
| prendre | prendr- | je prendrai |
| attendre | attendr- | j'attendrai |
| vendre | vendr- | je vendrai |

> Tip: the endings **-ai, -as, -a** look like the present tense of **avoir** (j'ai, tu as, il a) — a useful memory hook.

steps.title: Irregular stems
steps.body:
## Irregular stems in the futur simple

A set of very common verbs use a **different stem** (not the infinitive) but keep the same endings. You must memorize these:

| Infinitive | Futur stem | Example (je) |
|---|---|---|
| **etre** | ser- | je **ser**ai |
| **avoir** | aur- | j'**aur**ai |
| **aller** | ir- | j'**ir**ai |
| **faire** | fer- | je **fer**ai |
| **venir** | viendr- | je **viendr**ai |
| **pouvoir** | pourr- | je **pourr**ai |
| **voir** | verr- | je **verr**ai |
| **devoir** | devr- | je **devr**ai |
| **vouloir** | voudr- | je **voudr**ai |
| **savoir** | saur- | je **saur**ai |

### Classic English-speaker traps
- **aller**: English speakers write *j'allerai* (wrong). The stem is **ir-**: *j'**ir**ai*.
- **pouvoir / voir**: The double **-r-** (*pourrai, verrai*) is required — do not drop one.
- **-re verbs with a regular stem**: do not forget to drop the **-e**: *prendre → prendr-*, NOT *prendrer-*.

steps.title: When to use the futur simple
steps.body:
## Uses of the futur simple

### 1. General or distant future
*L'annee prochaine, nous voyagerons en France.* (Next year, we will travel to France.)

### 2. Predictions
*Demain, il fera beau.* (Tomorrow, the weather will be fine.)

### 3. Promises
*Je t'appellerai ce soir.* (I will call you tonight.)

## Contrast with the futur proche (A1 review)

In A1, you learned **futur proche** (aller + infinitive) for the **near future**:

| Structure | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **aller + infinitive** | near / imminent | *Je vais partir.* (I am going to leave.) |
| **futur simple** | general / distant | *Je partirai demain.* (I will leave tomorrow.) |

Both are correct; the futur simple is slightly more formal and suits events further ahead in time or more open-ended predictions.

steps.title: Match the verb to its futur stem

exercise.prompt: Match each infinitive to its futur simple stem.
Translate: *I will speak French tomorrow.* (parler is a regular -er verb)
exercise.hint: parler + ai (je ending) = je parlerai. No stem change for -er verbs.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I will go to the cinema on Saturday.* (aller has an irregular stem!)
exercise.hint: aller's futur stem is ir-, so: j'irai. NOT j'allerai.
distractors: Je vais aller au cinema samedi.
Je suis alle au cinema samedi.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the futur simple of *pleuvoir* (stem: pleuvr-) to complete the weather prediction.
Fill in the futur simple of *faire* (nous form) to complete the sentence.
exercise.hint: faire's futur stem is fer-. Nous ending = -ons, so: ferons.

steps.title: Build: She will come to the party

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *She will come to the party.*
exercise.hint: venir's futur stem is viendr-. Elle form: viendra.

steps.title: Build: You will be able to see the film

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *You (tu) will be able to see the film.*
exercise.hint: pouvoir's futur stem is pourr-. Tu form: pourras (double -r-).

exercise.prompt: Someone is making a distant promise: 'I will call you tonight.' Which sentence uses the futur simple correctly?
exercise.hint: A promise phrased as 'will' = futur simple. 'Going to' = futur proche.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *They will finish the work tomorrow.* (ils; finir is regular)
exercise.hint: finir + ont (ils ending) = finiront. No stem change for -ir verbs.
distractors: Ils vont finir le travail demain.
Ils finiront pas le travail demain.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *She will take the train.* (prendre is a -re verb — remember to drop the -e!)
exercise.hint: prendre → drop -e → prendr- + a (elle form) = prendra.

steps.title: Match the futur simple form to its meaning

exercise.prompt: Match each futur simple form to its English meaning.
